2604.02923.title: Council Mode: Mitigating Hallucination and Bias in LLMs via Multi-Agent Consensus
2604.02923.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly those employing Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures, have achieved remarkable capabilities across diverse natural language processing tasks. However, these models frequently suffer from hallucinations -- generating plausible but factually incorrect content -- and exhibit systematic biases that are amplified by uneven expert activation during inference. In this paper, we propose the Council Mode, a novel multi-agent consensus framework that addresses these limitations by dispatching queries to multiple heterogeneous frontier LLMs in parallel and synthesizing their outputs through a dedicated consensus model. The Council pipeline operates in three phases: (1) an intelligent triage classifier that routes queries based on complexity, (2) parallel expert generation across architecturally diverse models, and (3) a structured consensus synthesis that explicitly identifies agreement, disagreement, and unique findings before producing the final response. We implement and evaluate this architecture within an open-source AI workspace. Our comprehensive evaluation across multiple benchmarks demonstrates that the Council Mode achieves a 35.9% relative reduction in hallucination rates on the HaluEval benchmark and a 7.8-point improvement on TruthfulQA compared to the best-performing individual model, while maintaining significantly lower bias variance across domains. We provide the mathematical formulation of the consensus mechanism, detail the system architecture, and present extensive empirical results with ablation studies.
2604.02923.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs），特別是那些採用專家混合（MoE）架構的模型，在各種自然語言處理任務中取得了卓越的能力。<lb/>然而，這些模型經常遭受幻覺的困擾——生成看似合理但事實上不正確的內容——並且在推理過程中，由於專家激活的不均勻，表現出系統性偏見。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了Council Mode，一種新穎的多代理共識框架，通過並行地將查詢分派給多個異質的前沿LLM，並通過專門的共識模型合成它們的輸出，以解決這些限制。<lb/>Council管道分為三個階段： (1) 一個智能分流分類器，根據複雜性路由查詢， (2) 在架構多樣的模型中進行並行專家生成，以及 (3) 一個結構化的共識合成，明確識別一致性、分歧和獨特發現，然後生成最終回應。<lb/>我們在一個開源AI工作空間中實現並評估這一架構。<lb/>我們在多個基準上的全面評估顯示，Council Mode在HaluEval基準上實現了35.9%的相對幻覺率降低，並在TruthfulQA上相比於表現最佳的單一模型提高了7.8個點，同時在各個領域保持了顯著較低的偏見變異。<lb/>我們提供了共識機制的數學公式，詳細描述了系統架構，並呈現了大量的實證結果和消融研究。
2604.02923.author: Shuai Wu et.al.
2604.02923.authors: Shuai Wu, Xue Li, Yanna Feng, Yufang Li, Zhijun Wang
